data.narrative:
Precision Neuroscience is a Series C biotech company that has raised $180M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 public-market Biotech & Health comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Ultima Genomics, Insitro, and Colossal Biosciences at equivalent or nearby stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $180M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $306M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range of $1.5B (Colossal Biosciences) to $4.0B (Ultima Genomics). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable because the target's revenue is not disclosed, which is standard for pre-revenue or early-stage private biotech.

The triangulated valuation range is $260M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Precision Neuroscience's technology and market position more closely resemble earlier-stage peers or better-capitalized competitors like Ultima Genomics. Resolution depends on clinical progress, IP strength, and commercial traction—none of which are provided in available data.
